title: Using reduced representations well: visualization vs. preprocessing, misuse, and the boundary with Ch 15
key_concepts: Misuse 1: Using non-linear visualization tools (t-SNE/UMAP) as direct features for predictive models without understanding that distances in the new space do not represent true metric distances.
Misuse 2: Leakage. Fitting a dimensionality reduction model (like PCA) on the entire dataset before splitting train/test.

The Boundary with Chapter 15: Chapter 8 focuses on unsupervised compression of *your own data matrix*. Chapter 15 focuses on *transferring semantic representations* (pretrained embeddings) learned from massive external datasets.
callouts: Pipeline discipline (praktik pipeline yang benar): Explicitly state that reduction models must only be fitted on the training split, and then used to transform the inference/test split to prevent data leakage.
relations: Two-way cross-reference: Distinguish clearly from Chapter 15 to prevent confusion over neural techniques (autoencoder vs pretrained models). Connect to Chapter 2 (Leakage).
